description: The figure presents a temperature–time profile schematically illustrating the isothermal constant strain rate hot compression experimental procedure for Fe-Mn-Al-C steel (composition: 1.46 C, 25.14 Mn, 10 Al, 0.053 Nb, balance Fe) conducted on a Gleeble-3500 thermo-mechanical simulator using cylindrical specimens of Φ8 mm × 12 mm. The thermal cycle comprises: heating to 1200 °C at 10 °C/s, homogenization holding for 120 s, controlled cooling to the target deformation temperature (900–1150 °C) at 5 °C/s, an isothermal hold for 15 s to equalize internal and external temperature gradients, followed by compression to a maximum true strain of 0.92 (60% height reduction) at strain rates of 0.01, 0.1, 1, and 10 s⁻¹, and finally water quenching to retain the high-temperature deformed microstructure for subsequent metallographic analysis. The diagram explicitly labels each stage’s thermal parameter, emphasizing the 15-s holding step that ensures temperature uniformity prior to deformation and the quenching step that freezes the dynamic recrystallization state. This protocol underpins the rheological stress measurements and microstructural characterization used to construct constitutive models, DRX state diagrams, and grain size prediction models for the low-density high-strength steel.
